question: I am looking for a major Texas county that consistently ranked in the top five for childcare centers participating in food programs between 2018 and 2022. Among these leaders, find the one that reported over 600 child protective service removals in three years after the year (within 2017-2022) that has the highest total daily census records from Allegheny County Jail despite completing fewer than 14,000 abuse investigations. Compare this county's establishment date with Tarrant County's; for the earlier of the two, report the admission order number of the U.S. state admitted to the Union in that same year. Please return the order number only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Counting records by year from census_date for 2017-2022, the maximum total is 1202002 in 2019.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 has the highest total daily census records from Allegheny County Jail? (Use census_date to derive year; count records by year and select the maximum.)

2.fact: In 2018-2019, counts by SiteCounty show 195 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (1781), DALLAS (1207), HIDALGO (878), BEXAR (622), TARRANT (562).
2.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in 2018-2019? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2018-2019'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

3.fact: In 2019-2020, counts by SiteCounty show 199 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (2179), DALLAS (1435), HIDALGO (1203), BEXAR (888), CAMERON (592).
3.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in 2019-2020? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2019-2020'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

4.fact: In 2020-2021, counts by SiteCounty show 194 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (1709), DALLAS (1120), HIDALGO (918), BEXAR (756), TARRANT (592).
4.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in 2020-2021? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2020-2021'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

5.fact: In 2021-2022, counts by SiteCounty show 193 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (1871), DALLAS (1269), HIDALGO (924), BEXAR (772), TARRANT (687).
5.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in 2021-2022? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2021-2022'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

6.fact: In 2022-2023, counts by SiteCounty show 194 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (2183), DALLAS (1352), HIDALGO (947), BEXAR (743), TARRANT (715).
6.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in 2022-2023? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2022-2023'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

7.fact: In FY<node_1 answer + 3> (2022), among the top 5 counties by average CACFP sites, Harris (23,468) and Dallas (14,192) had more than 14,000 completed child abuse/neglect investigations, while Tarrant (13,286), Bexar (13,020), and Hidalgo (4,984) had less than 14,000.
7.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 2-6 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Hidalgo, Bexar, Tarrant> had less than 14,000 completed child abuse/neglect investigations in FY<node_1 answer + 3>? (Filter: Fiscal Year == <node_1 answer + 3>; sum Completed Investigations by County; keep < 14,000.)

8.fact: In FY<node_1 answer + 3> (2022), among the top 5 counties by average CACFP sites, Bexar (1,074) and Dallas (738) had more than 600 child protective service removals, while Harris (566), Tarrant (555), and Hidalgo (90) had 600 or fewer.
8.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 2-6 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Hidalgo, Bexar, Tarrant> had more than 600 CPS removals in FY<node_1 answer + 3>? (Filter: Fiscal Year == <node_1 answer + 3>; sum Removals by County; keep > 600.)

9.fact: According to Wikipedia, Bexar County 'was created on December 20, 1836' as one of the original counties of the Republic of Texas.
9.subquestion: When was <intersection of nodes 7-8: Bexar County> created?

10.fact: According to Wikipedia, Tarrant County was established in 1849.

11.fact: Arkansas was admitted to the Union as the 25th state on June 15, 1836.
11.subquestion: When was the 25th US state admitted to the Union?

reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=1, data.gov year derivation - node 1):
Node 1: Year with highest Allegheny County Jail daily census records (2017-2022) -> 2019
Derived year: 2022 (2019+3)
HOP 2 (d=5, data.gov AGGREGATION - nodes 2-6):
Node 2: CACFP Centers 2018-2019 -> site counts per county
Node 3: CACFP Centers 2019-2020 -> site counts per county
Node 4: CACFP Centers 2020-2021 -> site counts per county
Node 5: CACFP Centers 2021-2022 -> site counts per county
Node 6: CACFP Centers 2022-2023 -> site counts per county
AGGREGATION: Compute average across all 5 years -> Top 5 are {Harris: 1944.6, Dallas: 1276.6, Hidalgo: 974.0, Bexar: 756.2, Tarrant: 628.8}
Note: All 5 years required because rankings vary year to year (e.g., Tarrant was #6 in 2019-2020)
HOP 3 (d=2, data.gov INTERSECTION - nodes 7-8):
Node 7: From top 5, investigations < 14,000 in 2022 -> {Tarrant, Bexar, Hidalgo}
Node 8: From top 5, CPS removals > 600 in 2022 -> {Bexar, Dallas}
INTERSECTION: {Tarrant, Bexar, Hidalgo} ∩ {Bexar, Dallas} = {Bexar}
Both criteria necessary: Node 7 alone gives 3 counties, Node 8 alone gives 2 counties
HOP 4 (d=2, Wikipedia AGGREGATION - nodes 9-10):
Node 9: Bexar County Wikipedia -> created 1836
Node 10: Tarrant County Wikipedia -> created 1849
AGGREGATION: Compare years -> earlier is 1836 (Bexar)
Both nodes needed: Node 9 provides Bexar creation year, Node 10 provides Tarrant creation year for comparison
HOP 5 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 11):
Node 11: Arkansas Wikipedia -> admitted June 15, 1836 as the 25th state
Result: 25th state
Final answer: 25
